Search for the book in Primo, click “Place Hold,” and sign in [1][3][5]. Then choose Oxford Campus / King Library as the pickup location and wait for the notification that it is ready for pickup [2][4].

No. On the Oxford campus, King Library has 3D printing in its Makerspace [1]. Middletown has 3D printing in its TEC Lab Makerspace [2].
I don't have a reliable answer to that. You can ask a librarian directly through Ask Us.

Miami University Libraries’ Digital Collections contain thousands of digital objects, including photographs, advertising tradecards, newspapers, manuscripts, and videos from Walter Havighurst Special Collections & University Archives [1]. Example collections listed on the page include the Postcard Collection, Online Civil War Diaries, Cradle of Coaches, Early Miami University History, and the Frank Snyder Photograph Collection [3]. You can explore the front door at https://www.lib.miamioh.edu/digital-collections/ [1].
I can't place holds on items. Please place holds through your library account at https://www.lib.miamioh.edu/ or ask a librarian for help.

Faculty/staff can download Adobe Acrobat Pro after checking out a license on the Software page [5]. After checkout, you log in at adobe.com using your Miami credentials to download it to your device [5].

Check the MakerSpace equipment page for the current list of tools, including whether a vinyl cutter is available [1]. The King Library MakerSpace page says the MakerSpace is a popular venue at King Library, and the equipment page is the place to verify current equipment [3][1].

Adobe Acrobat Pro is available through Miami University Libraries’ Software Checkout as part of Adobe Creative Cloud [1][4]. The Software Checkout page includes options for different user types (e.g., Adobe Creative Cloud for students and an Acrobat Pro option for faculty/staff) [4][5].
I can't submit ILL requests directly, but I can show you how to request items from other libraries.
I can't submit ILL requests directly, but I can show you how to request items from other libraries.
Walter Havighurst Special Collections & University Archives is Closed today (Saturday, 2026-05-23) [1]. Its hours for the week of 2026-05-23 are Closed every day [1].

Yes — study rooms at King can be booked through the King room reservations page [1]. For today’s availability, use the live booking/availability link on that page [1].
Yes, color printing is available. Check the Printing & WiFi page for the current instructions and details [1][2].
You can scan documents using the scanners at the multipurpose printers; see the Printing & WiFi page for current Oxford-library scanning details and instructions [1][2].

The sources point to the Oxford library food/drink policy page for the current rules on bringing food into the library [1][2][4]. One Oxford source also notes King Library is the flagship Oxford library, but the general food policy itself is handled on the policy page [5].
Yes — the libraries offer chargers for Mac, PC, and phones, along with other peripherals for checkout [1]. You can borrow them at a library checkout desk with your university ID [3].

Alea Henle was named head of the newly formed Access & Borrow department, which includes circulation [2]. The staff directory page is the place to look up the current head of circulation [1].
Find the biology librarian on the Liaisons page [1].
Find the history subject librarian on the Liaisons page [1].
Find the Biology subject librarian on the Liaisons page [1].
Yes—use the Libraries’ “Ask a Librarian” page to chat with a librarian online (it shows the current live chat status and chat hours) [1].
